titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Job Clark Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday July 17, 2020 at 07:02
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: Notebooks of an early student at the Yale Medical School (ca.1815) on lectures by Silliman, Ives & Smith; notebooks on his medical practice; medical reference and prescription notebooks; Ravenna, Ohio, meteorological journals; miscellaneous medical notes and printed material. An addition to the collection comprises personal correspondence of Job Clark and family members, as well as journal entries and writings.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Sheldon DeForest Clark, 1968; gift of Sheldon DeForest, Dr. Lucy Swanton Clark, and Jared A. Clark, 2011.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Job Clark Papers (MS 18). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright to the typed transcripts in Accession 2012-M-015 has been transferred to Yale University. Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Job Clark Papers consist of notes, writings, and printed material (1810-1858) relating primarily to the Yale Medical School and the practice of medicine. Clark is listed in the Catalogue of the Medical School of 1815. His medical notes on lectures by Benjamin Silliman, Eli Ives, and Nathan Smith, 1815-1816, are particularly valuable as they relate to the earliest period of medical school history--the Medical Institution was organized August 31, 1813. An addition to the collection focuses more broadly on the entire Clark family and comprises personal correspondence of its members, as well as journal entries and writings, describing their daily lives and interactions.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Job Clark grew up in Westfield, Massachusetts, and married Ruth Sheldon of that town. After attending the Yale Medical School in 1815 and 1816, he served as Surgeon's Mate with the Militia of Massachusetts until 1824. Clark later moved to Ravenna, Ohio, where he raised his family and practiced medicine until his death.

corpname.part: Yale University. School of Medicine -- History

did.unittitle: Meterorological Journal, Ravenna Portage Co., Ohio
Note on series of discourses on systematic theology
Admission cards to lectures in medicine and natural philosophy at Yale College
Invitation card to Commencement Ball of Williams College
Class notebooks on diseases and their treatment
Lecture notebook of medical instruction by Drs. Silliman, Smith, and Ives; 224 p.
Book of Reference and General Principles, indexed
Booklets and single pages with information related to his medical practice
Notebook of prescriptions and treatments, indexed

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by personal name, then by type of material, and chronologically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primary correspondents are Clark family members: Job and Ruth (Sheldon) Clark, son Charles, daughters Cornelia, Elizabeth, and Frances, and extended family members, and to a lesser extent, friends and acquaintances. The majority of the correspondence was identified by Lucy Swanton Clark and is in envelopes prepared by her with the name of the author, recipient, and date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presumably the handwriting of Elizabeth, includes copies of poems, rules for a game, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed transcriptions of journal entries were prepared by Lucy Swanton Clark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics are varied, including botany, intemperance, and sermon texts.

did.unittitle: Transcriptions of selected correspondence of family members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed transcriptions were prepared by Lucy Swanton Clark.
